author: han0110
content:
- On the SSE review, checked all the CLs, for those that separate beacon node and validator client, they have the validator client use SSE to get events from beacon node, so I think using SSE to integrate with the proof node would be okay (I'd start to call it proof node to avoid confusion)
- On the initial spec for proof node, did a draft here https://hackmd.io/@han/BJAmNVhu-g, the diff from eip8025 are mostly to make latency as small as possible, not sure if all make sense tho

author: taulepton_
content:
I'm having trouble providing feedback on the hackmd in the browser, so I will write it inline here. 
- Using HTTP instead of JSON-RPC makes sense.
- Using the new payload request root as a deterministic handle for proofs in combination with the proof type is objectively better than using the ProofGenId. I support this change.

I think the endpoint naming makes it appear to be a REST service rather than an RPC. Is this by design? For example, execution_proof_requests vs request_execution_proofs.

I wonder if, instead of having global SSE events, we could have proof generation request-scoped SSE events. It would work something like this:
POST /v1/execution_proof_requests -> new_payload_request_root
GET   /v1/execution_proof_requests/{new_payload_request_root} -> SSE stream

I'm not sure if request-scoped is better, but just a thought.

At a high level, I agree with the proposal. Nice work on this!

author: han0110
content:
> I think the endpoint naming makes it appear to be a REST service rather than an RPC. Is this by design? For example, execution_proof_requests vs request_execution_proofs.
Right, tried to make it standard or common, but no opinion on the naming. Can definitely do RPC verb naming.

> I wonder if, instead of having global SSE events, we could have proof generation request-scoped SSE events.
Thought about this, if the proof is attached in SSE, I think this'd be definitely better, to avoid unwanted proof being sent. But if the proof is not attached but a just a update on proof status, I think it'd be fine to subscribe to global SSEs

author: taulepton_
content:
> Right, tried to make it standard or common, but no opinion on the naming. Can definitely do RPC verb naming.

I don't have a strong preference here. Was just curious about the rationale.

> Thought about this, if the proof is attached in SSE, I think this'd be definitely better, to avoid unwanted proof being sent.

Is it possible to have multiple validators/beacon nodes use the same proof node. If so, then they may only want to subscribe to events related to requests that they initiate?

author: han0110
content:
> Is it possible to have multiple validators/beacon nodes use the same proof node.
Right, if multiple bn/vc share the same proof node, it makes sense to support subscribe events with a filter (e.g. `/v1/proof_events?new_payload_request_root=0x...`)

author: taulepton_
content:
Do you think it should be a totally different events endpoint or reuse the same endpoint prefix:
```
POST /v1/execution_proof_requests -> new_payload_request_root
GET  /v1/execution_proof_requests?new_payload_request_root={new_payload_request_root} -> SSE stream
```

author: taulepton_
content: I'm not sure what is preferable

author: taulepton_
content: I suppose if we keep it separate then we could introduce other event types in the future

author: han0110
content: That's what I thought so named it generic `/events`, but reusing the endpoint looks pretty straightforward actually. Perhaps we can generalize in the future when new event types come up?

author: taulepton_
content: I think we should proceed with your proposal

author: han0110
content: got it, will update the SSE API name and try to implement it in zkboost (just need some small api changes), and when the lighthouse 8025 branch is ready I can try to do the e2e test

author: taulepton_
content: The current lighthouse 8025 branch is ready to run this test

author: taulepton_
content: Well, we need to update the interface from JSON-RPC to HTTP + SSE but other than that its good to go.
